domain.definition: A geographic area owned or controlled by a single person or organization.

dominance.definition: The state of being dominant; of prime importance; supremacy.

drain.definition: A conduit allowing liquid to flow out of an otherwise contained volume; a plughole (UK)

drift.definition: (physical) Movement; that which moves or is moved.

driving.definition: To provide an impetus for motion or other physical change, to move an object by means of the provision of force thereto.

drown.definition: To die from suffocation while immersed in water or other fluid.

dual.definition: Of an item that is one of a pair, the other item in the pair.

dub.definition: To confer knighthood; the conclusion of the ceremony was marked by a tap on the shoulder with a sword.

dumb.definition: Unable to speak; lacking power of speech (kept in "deaf, dumb, and blind").

duo.definition: Two people who work or collaborate together as partners; especially, those who perform music together.

E.definition: The name of the Latin-script letter E.

ease.definition: Ability, the means to do something, particularly:

echo.definition: A reflected sound that is heard again by its initial observer.

ecological.definition: Relating to ecology, the interrelationships of organisms and their environment.

educator.definition: A person distinguished for his/her educational work, a teacher.

effectiveness.definition: The property of being effective, of achieving results.

efficiency.definition: The extent to which time is well used for the intended task.

ego.definition: The self, especially with a sense of self-importance.

elaborate.definition: To develop in detail or complexity

electoral.definition: Relating to or composed of electors.

elevate.definition: To raise (something) to a higher position.

elite.definition: A special group or social class of people which have a superior intellectual, social or economic status as, the elite of society.

embark.definition: To get on a boat or ship or (outside the USA) an aeroplane.

embarrassment.definition: A state of discomfort arising from bashfulness or consciousness of having violated a social rule; humiliation.

embassy.definition: The function or duty of an ambassador.

embed.definition: An embedded reporter or journalist, such as a war reporter assigned to and travelling with a military unit, or a political reporter assigned to follow and report on the campaign of a candidate.

embody.definition: To represent in a physical or concrete form; to incarnate or personify.

emergence.definition: The act of rising out of a fluid, or coming forth from envelopment or concealment, or of rising into view; sudden uprising or appearance.

empirical.definition: Pertaining to or based on experience.

empower.definition: To give permission, power, or the legal right to do something.

encompass.definition: To form a circle around; to encircle.

encouraging.definition: To mentally support; to motivate, give courage, hope or spirit.

endeavour.definition: A sincere attempt; a determined or assiduous effort towards a specific goal; assiduous or persistent activity.

endorse.definition: A diminutive of the pale, usually appearing in pairs on either side of a pale.

endorsement.definition: The act or quality of endorsing

endure.definition: To continue or carry on, despite obstacles or hardships; to persist.

enforce.definition: To keep up, impose or bring into effect something, not necessarily by force.

engagement.definition: An appointment, especially to speak or perform.

enrol.definition: To enter (a name, etc.) in a register, roll or list

enterprise.definition: A company, business, organization, or other purposeful endeavor.

enthusiast.definition: A person filled with or guided by enthusiasm.

entity.definition: That which has a distinct existence as an individual unit. Often used for organisations which have no physical form.

epidemic.definition: A widespread disease that affects many individuals in a population.

equation.definition: The act or process of equating two or more things, or the state of those things being equal (that is, identical).

erect.definition: Upright; vertical or reaching broadly upwards.

escalate.definition: To increase (something) in extent or intensity; to intensify or step up.

essence.definition: The inherent nature of a thing or idea.

establishment.definition: The act of establishing; a ratifying or ordaining; settlement; confirmation.

eternal.definition: One who lives forever; an immortal.

evacuate.definition: To leave or withdraw from; to quit; to retire from

evoke.definition: To call out; to draw out or bring forth.

evolutionary.definition: Of or relating to the biological theory of evolution.

exaggerate.definition: To overstate, to describe more than is fact.

excellence.definition: The quality of being excellent; brilliance

exceptional.definition: An exception, or something having an exceptional value

excess.definition: The state of surpassing or going beyond limits; the being of a measure beyond sufficiency, necessity, or duty; that which exceeds what is usual or proper

exclusion.definition: The act of excluding or shutting out; removal from consideration or taking part.

exclusive.definition: Information (or an artefact) that is granted or obtained exclusively.

exclusively.definition: (focus) to the exclusion of anything or anyone else; solely or entirely

execute.definition: To kill as punishment for capital crimes.

execution.definition: The act, manner or style of executing (actions, maneuvers, performances).

exile.definition: The state of being banished from one's home or country.

expenditure.definition: Act of expending or paying out.

exploitation.definition: The act of utilizing something; industry.

extremist.definition: A person who holds extreme views, especially one who advocates such views; a radical or fanatic.

F.definition: Used to profess respect for a deceased person, or for something that has been lost.

faction.definition: A group of people, especially within a political organization, which expresses a shared belief or opinion different from people who are not part of the group.

fade.definition: A golf shot that (for the right-handed player) curves intentionally to the right. See slice, hook, draw.

fairness.definition: The property of being fair or equitable.

fatal.definition: A fatality; an event that leads to death.

fate.definition: The presumed cause, force, principle, or divine will that predetermines events.

feat.definition: A relatively rare or difficult accomplishment.

felony.definition: A serious criminal offense, which, under United States federal law, is punishable by imprisonment for a term exceeding one year or by death.

feminist.definition: An advocate of feminism; a person who believes in bringing about the equality of the sexes (of women and men) in all aspects of public and private life

fibre.definition: A single piece of a given material, elongated and roughly round in cross-section, often twisted with other fibres to form thread.

fierce.definition: Exceedingly violent, severe, ferocious, cruel or savage.

filmmaker.definition: A producer or director of films/movies.

filter.definition: A device which separates a suspended, dissolved, or particulate matter from a fluid, solution, or other substance; any device that separates one substance from another.
